name: Phoenix lander
fact: First spacecraft to physically touch Martian water ice. Phoenix's robotic arm scooped ice that sublimated in front of its cameras within hours — direct visual confirmation of the subsurface ice that Mars Odyssey's neutron spectrometer had inferred from orbit. Operated 157 sols before the northern winter froze its solar panels.
capability: Validated water-ice-driven Mars exploration strategy; baselined polar-region surface operations.
